title: Superseding decisions across the graph
question: Should the model express one decision superseding or conflicting with another, beyond dependsOn and within-decision revision?
rationale: Stay informal — record relationships with dependsOn plus a plain rationale note (e.g. 'replaces d4'), and let the plan flow surface conflicts as advisory prose. No new relation type, which keeps out the whole-graph complexity d2 fought; informal handling covered the consult work (d21-d24).

tradeoffs.note: captures 'this decision replaces that one' across separate decisions
a new relation to model and render; risks the graph creep d2 fought
surfaces unresolved tension without one decision killing the other
fuzzy semantics — when is it a conflict vs. just a tradeoff?
no new concepts; change is modeled inside a decision's history
can't express 'X made separate decision Y obsolete'
zero new model; say it in existing fields
invisible to the viewer and not queryable
